titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Hannah Murphy, 6 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926), the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne. It was a reply written to Hannah Murphy, in the letter O'Farrell refers Murphy to the Chief Secretarys decision that her children must remain in school.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3922

correspAction.placeName: 14, Francis Street, Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
No. 3922. 6th March, 1916. Madam, In reply to your letter of the 21st ultimo
I have to refer you to the Chief Secretary's decision
communicated to you on the 4th February, that the
children must remain in the Schools. I am,
Madam,
Your obedient Servant, EO Farrell
Mrs Hannah Murphy, 14, Francis Street, Tralee, Co. K E R R Y.
